title: <div>  1.  Plot Breakdown – Summarize the entire story in 5-7 bullet points.</di
content:
1. Plot Breakdown – Summarize the entire story in 5-7 bullet points.
2. Character Decisions – How does the protagonist change based on the decisions they make?
3. Pacing & Structure – When does tension rise? When does it fall?
4. Scene Study – Pick one powerful scene and analyze:
• What happens?
• What changes from beginning to end?
• What emotion is driving the scene?

Activity: Create an “Influence Collage”
• List 3-5 works that inspire your project.
• For each one, write what you love most about it.
• Identify patterns—are there common themes, structures, aesthetics that connect them?
• Now ask: How do I make these elements my own?

4. The Positive and Negative Pulse (Momentum in Storytelling)

Stories move through contrast—positive and negative shifts keep a reader engaged.
• Not good vs. evil. Instead, think of how momentum changes scene by scene.
• A scene should start in one state and end in another.
context: From document: I’ve taken all of this into account and structured it into a logical… — chunk 5 (chunk 5/8)
